articles.title: Gemini gets notebooks to help you organize projects
articles.summary: Google Gemini introduces 'notebooks' feature to organize project information in one place, allowing users to consolidate files, conversations, and instructions for AI context.

articles.title: OpenAI made economic proposals — here’s what DC thinks of them
articles.summary: OpenAI presented economic proposals to Washington DC policymakers, though specific details about their reception remain unclear. The proposals appear aimed at shaping AI regulation and policy in the nation's capital.

articles.title: Meta is reentering the AI race with a new model called Muse Spark
articles.summary: Meta launched Muse Spark, its new AI model powering Meta AI app and website in the US. The model will soon integrate across WhatsApp, Instagram, Facebook, Messenger, and Meta's smart glasses.

articles.title: Poke makes using AI agents as easy as sending a text
articles.summary: Poke enables everyday users to access AI agents through simple text messages, automating tasks without requiring technical knowledge or complex setup.

articles.title: AWS boss explains why investing billions in both Anthropic and OpenAI is an OK conflict
articles.summary: AWS leadership defends investing billions in competing AI companies Anthropic and OpenAI, citing the company's established culture of managing competitive relationships with partners.

articles.title: Tubi is the first streamer to launch a native app within ChatGPT
articles.summary: Tubi launches the first native streaming app integration within ChatGPT, allowing users to access movies and shows directly through the AI chatbot.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a San Francisco cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure competing with AWS and Google Cloud.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges up to $200/month for AI coding assistance, but Goose offers identical functionality for free as an open-source alternative that runs locally without subscription fees.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign using AI tokens and coding challenges. The creative recruitment stunt decoded to a Berghain nightclub algorithm puzzle that attracted thousands of talented engineers.

articles.title: Iran-linked hackers disrupt operations at US critical infrastructure sites
articles.summary: Iranian government-backed hackers are disrupting US critical infrastructure by targeting programmable logic controllers in factories, water treatment plants, and refineries. Multiple US agencies warned of ongoing attacks since March 2026.

articles.title: Thousands of consumer routers hacked by Russia's military
articles.summary: Russia's military intelligence hacked 18,000-40,000 consumer routers globally to redirect users to credential-harvesting sites for espionage. The APT28 group targeted MikroTik and TP-Link devices across 120 countries.

articles.title: OpenClaw gives users yet another reason to be freaked out about security
articles.summary: OpenClaw, a popular AI agentic tool with 347,000 GitHub stars, poses security risks by requiring broad system access. A recently patched vulnerability highlights dangers of granting such extensive permissions.

articles.title: Mustafa Suleyman: AI development won’t hit a wall anytime soon—here’s why
articles.summary: Mustafa Suleyman argues AI development won't plateau soon, highlighting exponential growth trends that humans struggle to comprehend. Training data for frontier AI models has grown 1 trillion times since 2010, defying linear expectations.

articles.title: The Download: water threats in Iran and AI’s impact on what entrepreneurs make
articles.summary: Desalination plants in Iran face vulnerability as Trump threatens to destroy them if the Strait of Hormuz isn't reopened, potentially devastating water supplies across the Middle East region.

articles.title: Desalination plants in the Middle East are increasingly vulnerable
articles.summary: Middle Eastern desalination plants face escalating vulnerability amid regional conflicts, with Iran accusing the US of attacking a facility supplying water to 30 villages in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: ALTK‑Evolve: On‑the‑Job Learning for AI Agents
articles.summary: ALTK-Evolve enables AI agents to learn and improve during deployment through on-the-job training. This approach allows agents to adapt and enhance their capabilities based on real-world experience.

articles.title: Safetensors is Joining the PyTorch Foundation
articles.summary: Safetensors, a popular file format for storing machine learning models safely, is joining the PyTorch Foundation. This move strengthens its integration with the broader PyTorch ecosystem.

articles.title: Welcome Gemma 4: Frontier multimodal intelligence on device
articles.summary: Google launches Gemma 4, a frontier multimodal AI model designed to run efficiently on devices. It enables advanced intelligence capabilities for on-device processing.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI development and emerging trends in artificial intelligence technology.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing strategies.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft released an open-source toolkit for its 'farm of the future' initiative, aimed at advancing agricultural technology through AI and digital tools.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google introduces Flex and Priority inference tiers for the Gemini API to balance cost efficiency with latency performance, giving developers more flexible options.

articles.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
articles.summary: Google Vids now offers free AI-powered video creation and editing using Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1 technologies, enabling high-quality video generation at no cost.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google partnered with the Brazilian government to create a satellite imagery map designed to help protect the country's forests from deforestation and environmental damage.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, its most capable open-source models designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows, offering improved intelligence byte-for-byte.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live improves voice interactions with enhanced precision and lower latency for more natural and fluid audio AI conversations.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind researches how AI can manipulate people in finance and health sectors, implementing new safety measures to prevent harmful exploitation.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and higher per-token prices. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family, with discussion of Mamba 3 and attention residuals in AI architecture.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia unveiled DLSS 5, an AI-powered graphics technology that uses generative AI to enhance photorealism in real-time gaming up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting conditions.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: AI podcast discussing major developments including Perplexity's local Mac AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub code review pricing, Cursor's coding automations, and new interactive visualization features from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: AI’s software development success and central management needs
articles.summary: AI has entered early production for many enterprises, but adoption is outpacing governance. Companies must establish better controls and integrate AI systems into existing platforms to safely manage agentic strategies.

articles.title: Microsoft open-source toolkit secures AI agents at runtime
articles.summary: Microsoft released an open-source toolkit for runtime security of AI agents in enterprises. It addresses governance challenges as autonomous AI models execute code and access corporate systems faster than traditional controls.

articles.title: Asylon and Thrive Logic bring physical AI to enterprise perimeter security
articles.summary: Asylon and Thrive Logic partner to combine autonomous security robots with AI analytics for enterprise perimeter security, enabling real-time incident response and automated workflows.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, addressing debugging challenges in collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduces Astra, a dual-model architecture designed to improve autonomous robot navigation by addressing localization, self-positioning, and pathfinding challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights via reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As LLMs become commoditized, sophisticated engineering harnesses around them are becoming the true competitive advantage, as revealed by Anthropic's Claude Code leak.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to steal credentials from developers running local OpenClaw AI agents on Mac Minis. The threat targets the growing boom of AI agent deployment on consumer hardware.

articles.title: Why Meta’s V-JEPA 2.1 model is a massive step forward for real-world AI
articles.summary: Meta's V-JEPA 2.1 advances video self-supervised learning by balancing motion tracking with spatial detail, addressing a historical limitation in AI models.

articles.title: Google AI Research Introduces PaperOrchestra: A Multi-Agent Framework for Automated AI Research Paper Writing
articles.summary: Google's PaperOrchestra is a multi-agent AI system that automatically converts rough research notes and experimental data into publication-ready LaTeX manuscripts with literature reviews and citations.

articles.title: A Comprehensive Implementation Guide to ModelScope for Model Search, Inference, Fine-Tuning, Evaluation, and Export
articles.summary: ModelScope tutorial covers end-to-end AI workflows including model search, fine-tuning sentiment classifiers, evaluation, and deployment. Demonstrates practical implementation on Colab with NLP and computer vision tasks.

articles.title: Meet OSGym: A New OS Infrastructure Framework That Manages 1,000+ Replicas at $0.23/Day for Computer Use Agent Research
articles.summary: OSGym is a new OS infrastructure framework that manages 1,000+ operating system replicas for AI agent research at just $0.23/day, solving the infrastructure challenge of training computer-using AI agents.

articles.title: Why I stopped using 'Modern Standby' on my Windows laptop to save battery overnight
articles.summary: Modern Standby mode may not effectively preserve Windows laptop battery overnight. Traditional sleep mode might be a better alternative for battery preservation.

articles.title: KDE Linux is the purest form of Plasma I've tested - but the install isn't for the meek
articles.summary: KDE Linux offers the purest version of KDE Plasma compared to other Linux distros that customize it. However, the installation process is challenging for inexperienced users.

articles.title: I found a hidden Google Photos tool that makes clearing storage feel less like a chore
articles.summary: Google Photos introduced a new swipe-based tool for Android users that simplifies deleting photos and freeing up storage space, making the process more intuitive and less tedious.

articles.title: A Hands-On Guide to Testing Agents with RAGAs and G-Eval
articles.summary: Guide to testing AI agents using RAGAs and G-Eval frameworks for evaluation. Covers hands-on methods for assessing agent performance and quality metrics.

articles.title: Handling Race Conditions in Multi-Agent Orchestration
articles.summary: Race conditions occur when multiple agents simultaneously write to the same resource, causing conflicting outputs. Proper orchestration and synchronization mechanisms are essential to prevent data corruption and inconsistencies.

articles.title: Top 5 Reranking Models to Improve RAG Results
articles.summary: Reranking models improve RAG system results by better ordering retrieved documents. The article explores top five reranking models that enhance retrieval-augmented generation performance.

articles.title: Why AI Is Training on Its Own Garbage (and How to Fix It)
articles.summary: AI models are increasingly training on their own generated content, creating degraded data quality. Solutions focus on filtering synthetic data and prioritizing high-quality original sources.

articles.title: Detecting Translation Hallucinations with Attention Misalignment
articles.summary: Researchers present a cost-effective method for detecting hallucinations in neural machine translation by analyzing attention misalignment to estimate token-level uncertainty.

articles.title: How to Use Claude Code to Build a Minimum Viable Product
articles.summary: Learn how to use Claude Code, an AI coding agent, to efficiently build and present minimum viable products for your ideas.

articles.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
articles.summary: NVIDIA highlights physical AI breakthroughs during National Robotics Week, showcasing robots transforming industries. Advances in robot learning, simulation, and foundation models enable faster real-world deployment from virtual training.

articles.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
articles.summary: Google and NVIDIA optimized Gemma 4 models for local AI execution across devices, from RTX PCs to data centers, enabling on-device agentic AI with real-time context access.

articles.title: Press Start on April: GeForce NOW Brings 10 Games to the Cloud
articles.summary: GeForce NOW launches 10 new games in April, including Capcom's PRAGMATA and Arknights: Endfield, a 3D real-time strategy game expanding the acclaimed series.

articles.title: Customize Amazon Nova models with Amazon Bedrock fine-tuning
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock now enables customization of Amazon Nova models through fine-tuning, allowing businesses to adapt AI models for specific needs like brand voice, industry workflows, and intent classification.

articles.title: Human-in-the-loop constructs for agentic workflows in healthcare and life sciences
articles.summary: AI agents in healthcare require human-in-the-loop oversight to ensure regulatory compliance and patient safety. AWS services enable practical implementation of these essential constructs.

articles.title: Building intelligent audio search with Amazon Nova Embeddings: A deep dive into semantic audio understanding
articles.summary: Amazon Nova Multimodal Embeddings enable intelligent audio search by converting audio into numerical vectors that capture acoustic features like tone, emotion, and environmental sounds, overcoming limitations of text-based transcription approaches.

articles.title: Integrate Physical AI Capabilities into Existing Apps with NVIDIA Omniverse Libraries
articles.summary: NVIDIA Omniverse Libraries enable developers to integrate Physical AI capabilities into existing applications, allowing teams to design and validate robots in simulated environments before deployment.

articles.title: Running AI Workloads on Rack-Scale Supercomputers: From Hardware to Topology-Aware Scheduling
articles.summary: NVIDIA's GB200 and GB300 NVL72 supercomputers feature Blackwell architecture with 18 compute trays and high-bandwidth networking for efficient AI workload execution through topology-aware scheduling.

articles.title: Accelerating Vision AI Pipelines with Batch Mode VC-6 and NVIDIA Nsight
articles.summary: NVIDIA accelerates vision AI pipelines using batch mode VC-6 and Nsight tools to optimize decode, preprocessing, and GPU scheduling stages alongside improved model throughput.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study examines how different generations perceive social media advertisements across various platforms, revealing generational differences in consumer attitudes toward ads.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to achieving net zero emissions by 2030, developing carbon footprint mitigation technology and sharing it openly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with a non-traditional background developed cutting-edge extended reality technology, demonstrating how unconventional paths can lead to innovative breakthroughs in emerging tech fields.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers present methods for identifying interactions at scale in Large Language Models to improve interpretability and transparency. The work analyzes LLM behavior through feature attribution to enhance AI safety and trustworthiness.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven design of imaging systems that uses an encoder to map objects to images, corrupted by noise. An information estimator quantifies measurement quality using only noisy data and noise models.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new RL algorithm uses divide-and-conquer instead of temporal difference learning, avoiding TD's scalability issues and improving performance on long-horizon tasks.
